steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. It asks for the main purpose of the entire passage. The text presents a long-standing hypothesis (Clovis points caused mammoth extinction), then describes an experiment designed to test it, and concludes that the results undermined the hypothesis. The structure is: hypothesis → experiment → results that challenge it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To argue for the significance of new findings amid an ongoing debate among researchers.': The passage does not frame the findings as part of an ongoing debate among multiple researchers — there is one anthropologist and one hypothesis. No broader debate is described. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the method used in an experiment.': The passage never evaluates whether the mechanical spear thrower or clay mounds were good or bad substitutes. No methodological critique appears. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To summarize two competing hypotheses and a major finding associated with each one.': Only one hypothesis is presented (Clovis-point hunting). There is no second hypothesis in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To describe an experiment whose results cast doubt on an established hypothesis.': The passage introduces an established hypothesis (Clovis points drove extinction), describes the experiment, and ends with the finding that spears likely weren't the principal drivers — directly casting doubt on that hypothesis. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
